preamble: The nasalized vowel [ã] reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed syllables through stress-conditioned vowel reduction. This process is documented in Romance and other languages, where nasalization is lost as the vowel centralizes to schwa in weak positions.

examples.note: Nasalized vowels reduce to schwa in unstressed positions in rapid French speech, losing nasalization in the process.
Reduction of nasalized [ã] in unstressed contexts, with denasalization accompanying the vowel reduction.
